definition: if something is affordable, it has a reasonable price that is not too expensive, so most people would be able to buy it
example: they’re looking for an affordable flat
we can’t afford a holiday abroad on my salary

definition: in a house, the attic is the room just underneath the roof, often used for storing things
example: we keep all sorts of rubbish in the attic

definition: a basement is a room or area in a building that is below the level of the ground outside
example: the basement was flooded again last week

definition: if something is compact, it is small and does not use much space
example: the flat is nice and compact, so it’s easy to keep clean

definition: something that is convenient helps you to do things easily. For example, if a place is convenient for the station, it is near the station so you can get there easily. If it is convenient to do something, then you can do it without any problems
example: the hotel was in the city centre, very convenient for the museums
the house is conveniently situated for the local shops
I enjoy the convenience of e-books, but still prefer paper copies
having to remember two passwords is really inconvenient
the low ceilings are an inconvenience for people over six feet tall

definition: if a place is cramped, there is not enough space and it is uncomfortable to live or work there
example: the house is quite cramped with four of us living here

definition: if you envy someone, you think they are lucky to have something or be doing something and wish you could have it or do it too
example: I don’t envy them all that work they have to do
he never seemed to excite envy in his colleagues
collocations: be envious of someone or something

example: she was envious of my financial success
collocations: be envious of someone or something

example: it was not an enviable task and I was glad Alec volunteered for it
collocations: be envious of someone or something

definition: to face someone or something or to face a particular direction means to be opposite them or to be looking in that direction
example: she turned to face the class
translation: быть обращенным (к чему-то), смотреть в сторону

definition: fire is the heat and flames are made when things burn
example: an open fire (in which coal or wood is burned inside a room)

definition: the heating in a building is the equipment that provides heat for the building
example: the house has got gas central heating
how much does it cost to heat this place?

definition: if a place or person has potential, there is a big possibility that they can develop and become different or better in the future
example: the house has got real potential
he is thought of as a potential prime minister
a potentially lucrative (well-paid) contract

definition: the roof of a building is the top surface that stops rain coming in
example: the roof is leaking

definition: a building that is run-down is not in good condition because it is old and hasn’t been looked after well. You can also say that an area of a town or city is run-down
example: when we bought the house, it was old and run-down

definition: if things are separate, they are not together or joined to each other
example: we wanted the children to have separate rooms
always wash coloured clothes separately from the whites
if two students are talking, the teacher might separate them

definition: if a room or building is spacious, it is big and has a lot of space in it
example: the rooms are light and spacious

definition: a floor, roof, or wall that is tiled is covered in square, flat pieces of baked clay
example: the kitchen has a tiled floor
we’re going to tile the lower half of the walls
we’re going to tile the lower half of the walls

definition: conflict or a conflict is violent fighting between groups of people or countries
example: in any armed conflict there will be civilian casualties

definition: if prices crash, they drop a lot very quickly because people suddenly stop buying things or do not want to pay so much money for them. If a country’s economy crashes, it is badly affected and starts to fail
example: there’s a risk that house prices will crash

definition: if you experience a particular situation or problem, it happens to you or affects you
example: do you experience difficulty concentrating?

definition: if something fails to happen, it does not happen. If you fail to do something, you do not do it
example: over 70% fail to reach their target
translation: не суметь, не справиться, не удаваться (сделать что-то)

example: he was criticised for his failure to communicate with his colleagues

definition: a gap is a very big difference between two situations, groups of people, or ideas
example: the growing gap between the rich and the poor (the rich are getting richer and the poor are getting poorer)

definition: your goals are the things you want to achieve in your life
example: my goal is to be a senior lecturer by the time I’m 35

definition: to hit a particular level on a scale means to reach it
example: the housing market has hit the bottom

definition: immigration is the process in which people move into a country from another country in order to live and work there
example: there’s been a sharp rise in immigration

definition: an immigrant is someone who has left their own country and now lives in another country
example: since 1970, the immigrant population has doubled

definition: investment is the process of buying shares in a company in the hope that they will pay you money regularly from their profits. An investment is also something you buy now because you think it will increase in value
example: he works as an investment analyst
should I invest in property?

definition: an investor is someone who buys shares in a company in the hope that they will receive money regularly from the profits. An investor is also someone who buys something because they think it will increase in value
example: investors from abroad are buying up a lot of property

definition: you use largely when introducing the main reason for something, although there are also other reasons
example: it was largely because of the economic problems in neighbouring countries

definition: the middle class is one of the groups which people in a society are divided into according to their jobs, education, background, etc. People from that class are middle-class
example: wages are increasing for middle-class people
the middle classes were badly affected by the tax rises

definition: if you pray, you speak to the god that you believe in. To pray for something also means to hope very much that it will happen
example: he went into the church to pray

definition: privacy is a state in which no one can see you or know what you are doing
example: many people will fight to protect their privacy
she’s a very private person (she never says much about herself to anyone)

definition: property is land and buildings
example: property prices are high in London

definition: if people protest, they say very forcefully that they do not agree with a plan or situation
example: many local people protested about the new building plans
a mass protest by students
a mass protest by students

definition: the rate at which something happens is the number of times it happens over a certain period of time
example: the crime rate is going down finally

definition: something that is rising is getting bigger in number, size, or value
example: they’re enjoying better conditions and rising salaries
we expected to see a rise in the number of students
we expected to see a rise in the number of students

definition: a sharp change in the rate or size of something is a sudden and big change in it
example: a sharp rise in immigration
prices rose sharply

definition: if there is a shortage of something, there is not enough of it
example: there is a shortage of housing in the UK
I’m a bit short of cash at the moment. Can you lend me £10?

definition: if a process or activity slows down, it starts to happen less actively. If a country’s economy slows down, there is less economic activity and businesses start to fail and people lose their jobs
example: the market is beginning to slow down
Africa is feeling the full impact of the global economic slowdown

definition: if something changes steadily, it changes constantly and gradually, and usually at the same rate
example: house prices have been rising steadily for the last ten years
a steady increase in prices

definition: a target is something you are trying to achieve in your life or an objective you have as part of your job
example: over 70% of students fail to reach their earnings target

definition: access to somewhere or something is the fact of being able to go there or use it
example: compact room with access to own kitchen facilities
guests can access several tennis courts and the swimming pool

definition: the authorities are official institutions such as central and local government who are able to make laws and decide how a country, town, or city should be run
example: the authorities have done a lot to reduce the crime rate

definition: an awkward situation is one in which people feel embarrassed because it is difficult for them all to do what they want to do, and they do not want to make things difficult for the others
example: it’s a bit awkward with three people trying to use the kitchen at once
we shook hands a little awkwardly, then left

definition: if one thing covers something else, it is on top of the other thing so that it is difficult to see what is underneath
example: cover the table with a cloth before you start painting on it

definition: if a place is dead, it is not very lively
example: it’s pretty dead in the centre after 8 in the evening

definition: something that is decorated has been made to look beautiful by being painted or by having pretty things added to it
example: a huge and beautifully decorated house
it took us all weekend to decorate the downstairs of the house
the decoration in the bedrooms was a bit tired and needed redoing

definition: the distance between two things is the amount of space between them. If something is in the distance, you can see it or hear it, but it is a long way from where you are
example: our flat is within walking distance of the college (it is easy to walk there)
he heard the distant sound of the motorway (the sound was coming from a long way away)

definition: if something is downstairs, it is on the ground floor level of a building or on a level below where you are
example: both the tables are needed downstairs
the three downstairs rooms were all flooded

definition: if something is filthy, it is extremely dirty
example: the streets were filthy and covered in litter
everything was covered in filth

definition: an isolated place is far away from other buildings, shops, towns etc. If you feel isolated, you are lonely because you are in place that is a long way from your friends or family
example: you’ll be a bit isolated without a car
I didn’t like the isolation of working from home

definition: litter is rubbish that is dropped in public places and which makes those places look untidy
example: the streets are covered in litter

definition: a place that is multicultural has people from a lot of different countries and cultures living and working together
example: London is so much more multicultural than it was in the 1930s

definition: if you are naked, you have no clothes on
example: you could go around naked and no one would see you

definition: to offer something means to have it available for people, either as part of a service or as part of what someone is buying or renting
example: large, old house offering quiet, comfortable accommodation

definition: if you settle somewhere, you start to live there permanently
example: a lot of immigrants have settled here recently
European settlers started to arrive in 1793

definition: a tenant is someone who pays rent to live in a house or flat, or in a room in a house or flat
example: Anastasia is looking for a tenant
